rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 4, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF STEVE RYAN Notice Of Motion By Plaintiff Steve Ryan To Disqualify Counsel Mitchell Rosenfeld Granted in part and denied in part. Granted as to Defendants Sachiko Rosenfeld, Michael Sorantino and Moejoe Properties, LLC as the evidence shows Mitchell Rosenfeld previously represented Plaintiff in a real estate dispute. rulings.Case Title: LANA WONG VS. JOHNSON, DANIEL INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA STONE INK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION (Joinder) In Deft Pacific Heights Towers Condominium Association'S Motion
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 4, 2014, Line 17, CROSS COMPLAINANT CITISCAPE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT GROUP, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION (Joinder) In Deft Pacific Heights Towers Condominium Association'S Motion Granted in part and denied in part. As to the cause of action for violation of civil rights (Civ. Code sec. 51), summary adjudication is granted. Defendant met its burden by proffering Plaintiff's discovery responses, which show that Plaintiff cannot set forth specific facts establishing that a substantial motivating factor for Defendant's conduct was Plaintiff's race, national origin, or gender. (Material Fact ("MF") nos. 1-2; CACI 3060; Union Bank v. Sup. Ct. (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 573, 590, holding that a moving defendant may rely on factually devoid discovery responses to shift the burden of proof.) Plaintiff did not meet her burden showing there is a triable issue of material fact. Plaintiff contends that it can be inferred from circumstantial evidence of disparate treatment that Defendant had a discriminatory motive. However, Plaintiff offers no evidence beyond speculation. Further, in reply, Defendant introduces evidence showing that there was a legitimate non-discriminatory reason for the disparate treatment. (Supplemental Declaration of Catherine A. Salah, Exs. E and J.) Defendant also introduces evidence showing that Defendant was reimbursed for the funds it incurred in remediating Unit 1304. (Supp. Decl., Exs. E and I.) The fact that Defendant paid for remediating Unit 1304, but not Plaintiff's unit, is one of the primary facts that Plaintiff argues allows for the inference of discriminatory motive; however, Defendant's evidence indicates this is not the case. The Court may properly consider evidence submitted in reply because Plaintiff did not object to such consideration. (See Gafcon, Inc. v. Ponsor & Assocs. ((2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 1388, 1426, holding that although the moving party may generally not rely on additional evidence filed with its reply papers, an opposing party must object to the submission of such evidence or else it waives that right.) As to the cause of action for tort of another, summary adjudication is denied. As held in the June 6, 2012 Order, "[t]he tort of another doctrine is not a distinct cause of action, but merely allows the recovery of attorney's fees as damages." Under CCP § 437c(s), "a claim for damages other than punitive damages" may be determined by summary adjudication only if the parties jointly stipulate to the filing of the motion and the court orders that the motion may be filed. There is no such stipulation or order here. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/ehg

rulings.Case Title: LANA WONG VS. JOHNSON, DANIEL INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA STONE INK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 4, 2014, Line 17, DEFENDANT PACIFIC HEIGHTS TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Granted in part and denied in part. As to the cause of action for violation of civil rights (Civ. Code sec. 51), summary adjudication is granted. Defendant met its burden by proffering Plaintiff's discovery responses, which show that Plaintiff cannot set forth specific facts establishing that a substantial motivating factor for Defendant's conduct was Plaintiff's race, national origin, or gender. E and J.) Defendant also introduces evidence showing that Defendant was reimbursed for the funds it incurred in remediating Unit 1304. (Supp. Decl., Exs. E and I.) rulings.Case Title: LANA WONG VS. JOHNSON, DANIEL INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA STONE INK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On X-Complaint For Declaratory Relief Re Duty To Defend And Indemnity
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 4, 2014, Line 17, CROSS COMPLAINANT PACIFIC HEIGHTS TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On X-Complaint For Declaratory Relief Re Duty To Defend And Indemnity Cross-Complainant Pacific Heights Towers Condominium Association's ("PHT") motion for summary adjudication on the seventh cause of action for declaratory relief is granted. All requests for judicial notice are granted. PHT met its initial burden by establishing Cross-Defendant Amy McLaughlin ("McLaughlin") entered into multiple agreements providing that she would indemnify PHT for any and all claims arising out of construction work in her unit and adding PHT as an additional insured on required insurance policies in connection with the remodel work in her unit. (Undisputed Fact ("UF") nos. 5, 7-12.) Consequently, the burden shifted to McLaughlin to establish a triable issue of material fact. McLaughlin has failed to do so. As to the evidence presented showing that McLaughlin was not at fault for or a substantial factor in causing the damage to Plaintiff Lana Wong, this evidence is irrelevant given that the indemnification provisions are broadly written and do not require a showing of fault. (See Continental Heller Corp. v. Amtech Mechanical Services, Inc. (1997) 53 Cal.App.4th 500, 503; Centex Golden Construction Co. v. Dale Tile Co. (2000) 78 Cal.App.4th 992, 997.) The complaint in the underlying action alleges that she was damaged by construction dust from McLaughlin's apartment. McLaughlin has also not established a triable issue of material fact as to whether PHT was actively negligent. McLaughlin cites no legal authority holding that a Homeowner's Association has a duty to warn buyers about potential defects in a unit. (See Kovich v. Paseo Del Mar Homeowners' Assn. ((1996) 41 Cal.App.4th 863, 866-67.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/ehg

rulings.Case Title: LANA WONG VS. JOHNSON, DANIEL INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA STONE INK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re: Citiscape'S Cross Complaint Re: Second Cause Of Action For Indemnity Against Amy Mclaughlin
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 4, 2014, Line 17, DEFENDANT CITISCAPE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT GROUP, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re: Citiscape'S Cross Complaint Re: Second Cause Of Action For Indemnity Against Amy Mclaughlin. Cross-Complainant The Citiscape Property Management Group, LLC's ("Citiscape") motion for summary adjudication on the second cause of action for declaratory relief is granted. Citiscape met its initial burden by establishing Cross-Defendant Amy McLaughlin ("McLaughlin") entered into multiple agreements providing that she would indemnify Citiscape, as an agent and/or manager of Pacific Heights Towers Condominium Association, for any and all claims arising out of construction work in her unit. (Undisputed Fact ("UF") nos. 4, 8.) Consequently, the burden shifted to McLaughlin to establish a triable issue of material fact. McLaughlin has failed to do so. As to the evidence presented showing that McLaughlin was not at fault for or a substantial factor in causing the damage to Plaintiff Lana Wong, this evidence is irrelevant given that the indemnification provisions are broadly written and do not require a showing of fault. (See Continental Heller Corp. v. Amtech Mechanical Services, Inc. (1997) 53 Cal.App.4th 500, 503; Centex Golden Construction Co. v. Dale Tile Co. (2000) 78 Cal.App.4th 992, 997.) The complaint in the underlying action alleges that she was damaged by construction dust from McLaughlin's apartment. McLaughlin has also not established a triable issue of material fact as to whether Citiscape was actively negligent. McLaughlin cites no legal authority holding that a property management company has a duty to warn buyers about potential defects in a unit. (See Kovich v. Paseo Del Mar Homeowners' Assn. ((1996) 41 Cal.App.4th 863, 866-67.) The use of the word "agent" in the agreements is not ambiguous. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/ehg

rulings.Case Title: JOY EGU VS. WALGREENS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 4, 2014, Line 19, DEFENDANT WALGREENS CO., INCORRECTLY SUED AS WALGREENS DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT Overruled. The Third Amended Complaint ("TAC") sufficiently pleads facts explaining why the statement in the original Complaint is incorrect. (TAC, 11-16.) The TAC pleads that Plaintiff could not have reasonably discovered the wrongful cause of her injuries until March 22, 2012. (TAC, 11-16.) The filing of the complaint on February 26, 2013 was therefore within the one-year statute of limitations. (CCP sec. 340.5.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: 4EC HOLDINGS, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. LINEBARGER GOGGAN BLAIR & SAMPSON LLP, A TEXAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Cross-Motion And Cross-Motion For Protective Order Regarding The Confidentiality Of Documents And To Limit Or Defer Certain Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, March 4, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT LINEBARGER GOGGAN BLAIR & SAMPSON LLP's Cross-Motion For Protective Order Regarding The Confidentiality Of Documents And To Limit Or Defer Certain Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED EXCEPT TO THE EXTENT REFLECTED IN THE COURT'S RESPONSE TO PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL. ALTHOUGH THE COURT AGREES THAT THERE MAY WELL BE A JUSTIFICATION FOR THE ENTRY OF A PROTECTIVE ORDER, UNTIL SUCH TIME AS THERE IS A CLEARER ARTICULATION OF THE SPECIFIC DOCUMENTS THAT MIGHT BE SUBJECT TO SUCH AN ORDER THE ENTRY OF THE BROADLY WORDED ORDER PROPOSED BY DEFENDANT COULD PROVE TO BE CUMBERSOME, TIME CONSUMING AND COMPLICATING THE DISCOVERY AND TRIAL PROCESS UNNECESSARILY. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: 4EC HOLDINGS, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. LINEBARGER GOGGAN BLAIR & SAMPSON LLP, A TEXAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn 1) To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And To Req.'S For Production Of Documents And 2) For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft And It'S Attorney Of Record
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, March 4, 2014, Line 3, Plaintiff 4EC HOLDINGS, LLC's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And To Requests For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And it's Attorney Of Record (part 1/2 of tentative ruling). Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. 1. DEFENDANT LINEBARGER GOGGAN BLAIR & SAMPSON LLP ("LGBS") WILL FILE FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO.1 LIMITED TO IDENTIFYING THE PERSONS NAME & RESIDENCE ADDRESS. 2. DEFENDANT LGBS' OBJECTIONS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS 4, 5, 6, 9 & 12 ARE OVERRULED AND LGBS IS ORDERED TO PROVIDE FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES TO THEM. 3. DEFENDANT LGBS' OBJECTIONS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 7 & 8 ARE SUSTAINED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFF SEEKING SUCH INFORMATION IF AND WHEN CLASS CERTIFICATION IS GRANTED. Tentative ruling continued, see part 2/2. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: 4EC HOLDINGS, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. LINEBARGER GOGGAN BLAIR & SAMPSON LLP, A TEXAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff 4ec Holdings, Llc'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And To Requests For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And It'S Attorney Of Record (Part 2/2 Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, March 4, 2014, Line 3, Plaintiff 4ec Holdings, Llc'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And To Requests For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And It'S Attorney Of Record (Part 2/2 Of Tentative Ruling). 4. SUBJECT TO THE LIMITATIONS NOTED IN PARAGRAPH NO. 6 BELOW, DEFENDANT WILL PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 & 12. 5. PLAINTIFF HAS INDICATED THAT IT WOULD NOT REQUIRE A RESPONSE TO REQUEST NO. 7 IF LGBS WOULD FILE UNDER OATH THAT THE EXEMPLARS PROVIDED TO PLAINTIFF ARE REPRESENTATIVE OF THE LETTERS SENT TO CALIFORNIA RESIDENTS, AND DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO DO SO OR TO PROVIDE A RESPONSE CONSISTENT WITH PARAGRAPH 4 ABOVE; PLAINTIFF DID NOT REQUESTING ANY FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS NOS. 10 & 11. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUEST NO. 8 IS DENIED AT THIS TIME WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFF SEEKING SUCH INFORMATION IF AND WHEN CLASS CERTIFICATION IS GRANTED. 6. TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENDANT LGBS CONTENDS THAT ANY DOCUMENT RESPONSIVE TO THE REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION REFERRED TO IN PARAGRAPH 4 ABOVE IS SUBJECT TO AN ASSERTION OF PRIVILEGE ON ANY GROUND, WHETHER IT BE PRIVACY, ATTORNEY CLIENT COMMUNICATION OR ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT, IT WILL IDENTIFY THE DOCUMENT WITH SUFFICIENT PARTICULARITY TO ALLOW A COURT TO RULE ON THE VALIDITY OF SUCH OBJECTION. 7. ALL RESPONSES TO THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS WILL BE SERVED ON PLAINTIFF WITH FIFTEEN (15) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. 8. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED AT THIS TIME. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER REFLECTING THE TENTATIVE RULINGS AS TO BOTH HIS MOTION AS WELL AS DEFENDANT'S AS REFLECTED BELOW, SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR DEFNDANT FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNING AT THE HEARING ON MARCH 4, 2014. rulings.Case Title: JEREMY MARSH, NDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF ALL VS. REACHLOCAL, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Arb Of Pltf Marsh
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 4, 2014, Line 14, DEFENDANT REACHLOCAL, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION'S MOTION To Compel Arbitration Of Plaintiff Marsh Defendant's motion to compel arbitration and dismissal of class claims without prejudice is granted. The Court orders a stay of judicial proceedings as to Plaintiff's individual claims until arbitration is completed. All requests for judicial notice are granted. This Court recognizes a divergence between Federal and California case law on the issues raised in this motion. (Compare American Exp. Co. v. Italian Colors Restaurant (2013) 133 S.Ct. 2304; AT&T Mobility LLC v. Concepcion (2011) 131 S.Ct. 1740, with Gentry v. Superior Court (2007) 42 Cal.4th 443.) These issues are currently being reviewed by the California Supreme Court. (Iskanian v. CLS Transportation of Los Angeles, California Supreme Court Case No. S204032.) Even if this Court applied the standard under Gentry, Plaintiff has not shown that both procedural and substantively unconscionability exist to render the Arbitration Agreement unenforceable. Specifically, Plaintiff has not carried its burden of establishing substantive unconscionability under the four Gentry factors. (Gentry, supra, 42 Cal.4th at 457-64.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.
